title: Skill docs: explicit-tier transport wording + generation default for aux dispatches
action: Per D1/D3: (1) in skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md step 4 (~line 40), change the ceiling transport wording from 'inherit -> omit the Agent model param' to 'inherit -> omit the Agent model param AND carry the [bee-tier: ceiling] marker in the dispatch prompt or description (decision 0023 — a bare dispatch with neither param nor marker is denied by the model-guard hook)'; budget-type dispatches likewise carry [bee-tier: <tier>]. (2) In swarming-reference.md, update the resolveTier paragraph (~line 50) with the same marker rule, one sentence. (3) Add ONE sentence each to bee-planning (near the L2+ bee-xia dispatch guidance in section 2), bee-grooming, bee-scribing, bee-xia, bee-exploring SKILL.md where subagent/research dispatch is discussed: aux/ad-hoc subagent dispatches default to the generation slot (canonical fragment, verbatim in every aux skill so the verify can assert the contract: 'default to the generation slot'); dispatching at ceiling requires the [bee-tier: ceiling] marker plus a one-line justification (canonical fragment on ONE line: '[bee-tier: ceiling] marker plus a one-line justification'). In swarming-reference.md the budget clause must couple marker and budget in one sentence (one line containing both 'bee-tier' and 'budget'). Keep each skill's voice and formatting; these SOURCE skill files propagate to ~/.claude/skills on the next onboarding apply (cell 4). Where a skill already names a slot for a specific dispatch (exploring fresh-eyes review slot, validating plan-checker), leave that untouched — the sentence covers only otherwise-unspecified dispatches.

truths: The string 'bee-tier' appears in all 7 listed files
Exploring's fresh-eyes review-slot sentence and swarming's tier-judgment rubric (decision 0016) are semantically unchanged — only the transport wording moves
No skill outside the 7 listed files is touched
Each of the 5 aux skills states BOTH halves: generation default AND ceiling-needs-marker-plus-justification

swarming SKILL.md still contains its dispatch-time tier-judgment rubric sentence ('Judge each cell'), and exploring still names the review slot for fresh-eyes ('slot: `review`') — content-protection greps prove the existing rules survived

The two canonical fragments appear VERBATIM in each aux skill ('default to the generation slot'; '[bee-tier: ceiling] marker plus a one-line justification') — the verify asserts the contract, not keyword presence
prohibitions: No new sections or headings — one sentence per aux skill, in-place
No changes to bee-reviewing or bee-validating slot rules (already explicit)
No renaming of tiers or slots

verify: bash -c 'cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"; r=0; for f in skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md skills/bee-grooming/SKILL.md skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md skills/bee-xia/SKILL.md skills/bee-exploring/SKILL.md; do grep -Fq "default to the generation slot" "$f" || { echo "MISSING generation-default clause in $f"; r=1; }; grep -Fq "[bee-tier: ceiling] marker plus a one-line justification" "$f" || { echo "MISSING ceiling-justification clause in $f"; r=1; }; done; grep -Fq "[bee-tier: ceiling]" skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md || { echo MISSING-swarming-marker; r=1; }; grep -q "Judge each cell" skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md || { echo RUBRIC-LOST; r=1; }; grep -qiE "bee-tier.*budget|budget.*bee-tier" skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md || { echo MISSING-coupled-budget-clause; r=1; }; grep -q "slot: \`review\`" skills/bee-exploring/SKILL.md || { echo FRESH-EYES-LOST; r=1; }; node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs >/dev/null && echo LIB-OK || r=1; [ $r -eq 0 ] && echo PASS; exit $r'

trace.outcome: Added explicit-tier transport wording to bee-swarming (SKILL.md + reference) and one generation-default/ceiling-justification sentence to each of the 5 aux skills (planning, grooming, scribing, xia, exploring), per D1/D3; protected rubric and review-slot sentences left untouched.
